reasoning: The base rate expectation for a stock after 1 week, 1 month, and 3 months is roughly 51%, 53%, and 56% respectively based on market drift. However, TKO has just experienced an exceptional run-up in its stock price over the last two weeks, jumping from 175 to an all-time high area around 200.65 on Friday Dec 5. A statistical trend analysis estimates the chance of being above this specific threshold in a week at roughly 39%, reflecting high likelihood of short-term mean reversion or profit-taking. There is no major isolated news catalyst explicitly driving the immediate Dec 4 spike besides a $0.78 dividend announcement, although fundamental momentum is generally strong (Q3 guidance raised, EPS growth, WWE on Netflix starting Jan 2026). The ex-date for the dividend is likely Dec 12 or 15, which will mechanically drop the price by roughly $0.78, providing a very slight downward headwind for the short-to-medium term resolutions. Because of the strong mean reversion effect expected to act on an acute 10%+ spike over just a week, I place the 1-week probability heavily downwards towards 42%. Over 1 month (January 6), the effect of the Netflix deal kickoff could offer support, so I adjust probability to 46%. By March (3 months out), underlying market baseline and equity drift take over, putting the probability near balanced (51%) where a breakout could be sustained or reversion completes.
